title: Default Tool Setting for Account Login Redirect
story: As a team administrator, I want to set a default tool that loads when my team logs in, so that users are directed to the most relevant tool for our organization instead of always defaulting to the Dictionary.
description:
Add a 'Default Tool' setting on the account/team settings page that controls which tool loads when a user logs in. The setting is tied to the account/team level.

Behavior:
- First-time users default to the Dictionary tool
- Returning users who navigate to a specific tool URL while logged out are redirected there after login
- If a Default Tool is set for the account, logged-in users are always redirected to that tool
- Shared account users follow the shared account's Default Tool setting

The setting is available in the Settings page and persists across sessions.
acceptance_criteria: Default Tool dropdown on Settings page allows selecting any available tool
First-time users still default to Dictionary
Returning users are redirected to their intended tool if they navigated directly
Default Tool setting overrides the dictionary default on login
Shared accounts use the account-level Default Tool setting
ux_changes: Default Tool dropdown on Settings page. Login redirect respects the chosen default tool.
middleware_changes: Login redirect logic updated to check account-level default tool setting. First-time user detection preserved for Dictionary default.
data_model_changes: Default tool setting stored at the team/account level, one-to-one relationship with team.
implementation_notes: The feature was requested by a large prospective client. Implementation required no changes to the Salesforce callback URL or authentication flow — it simply modifies the post-login redirect target based on the account setting.
comment_summary: Developer quickly implemented with a settings dropdown and smart redirect logic. Handles first-time users, returning users with specific intent, and shared accounts. Ready for demo on development.
